body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed at 7,512.85, a slight improvement from 7,506.64 earlier in the day, but still below its starting position. Several indices saw gains, including LQ45 (up 1.28%), IDX30 (up 1.33%), IDX80 (up 0.85%), and IDXESGL (up 1.62%), while some stocks were corrected and others showed optimism.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Sees Volatile Trading Session with Mixed Performances.

body: BDDC is expanding its data centre presence, with its latest JST 1 centre in East Jakarta, and is preparing land for its next outer-city data centre in Cikarang, as part of its vision to establish 4 data centres within the next 3 years. The company's data centre scope now spans across Western Jakarta, South-East Jakarta, Eastern Jakarta, and is projected to expand to Central-North Jakarta, with a focus on providing a combination of inner- and outer-city data centres to ensure smooth data traffic in Indonesia.
title: BDDC to Expand Data Centre Presence in Jakarta with New Landbank in Cikarang

body: Bersama Digital Data Centre (BDDC) has launched its latest data centre, JST 1, in East Jakarta, with an initial capacity of 5 MW and 1,008 racks, requiring an investment of around USD 50-55 million. The company plans to increase the capacity to 32 MW, which will necessitate an investment of at least USD 320 million, or IDR 5 trillion, making it a significant long-term investment.
title: Bersama Digital Data Centre launches JST 1, a new 5-megawatt data centre in East Jakarta designed as a digital hub for Indonesia's digital sector.

body: The Indonesian government's auction of sharia securities or state sukuk last Tuesday received a total of IDR 14.81 trillion in incoming bids, with the highest bids recorded for the SPNS07072025 series at IDR 4.36 trillion and the PBS038 series at IDR 3.62 trillion. The total awarded amount for the 7 auctioned series was IDR 7.75 trillion, bringing the accumulated incoming bids since the beginning of 2024 to IDR 413.58 trillion and the awarded amount to IDR 177.62 trillion.
title: Indonesian Government's Sharia Securities Auction Sees IDR 14.81 Trillion in Incoming Bids

body: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi TBK's stock price surged 2,050% to IDR 7,050, boosting its market capitalization from IDR 2.47 trillion to IDR 79.25 trillion. The company's revenue and net profit also saw significant increases, with revenue up 347.82% to USD 309.69 million and net profit up 163.15% to USD 29.57 million in the first half of 2024 compared to the same period in 2023.
title: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi TBK's stock price surges 2050% following strong revenue and profit growth in first half 2024.

body: PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa TBK has invested IDR 2 billion, or 20% capital, in PT Amita Prakarsa Hijau, a joint venture with Japan's Amita Group, to produce alternative fuel materials from industrial and household waste. The investment, carried out through PT Sari Bhakti Sejati, aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions in cement production and utilizes alternative raw materials as fuel.
title: Indocement invests in joint venture to produce alternative fuel from industrial waste for cement manufacturers.

body: PT Mulia Boga Raya Tbk, a processed food manufacturer, has divested its entire treasury stock of 5.98 million shares at IDR 1,500 each, raising a total of IDR 8.98 billion. Following the divestiture, the company no longer holds any treasury shares, and its stock price has climbed 1.27% to IDR 1,595 per share, with a 38.70% increase since the beginning of the year.
title: PT Mulia Boga Raya sells entire treasury stock, raising IDR 8.98 billion.

body: PT Indika Indonesia Resources (IIR) has divested 60% of PT Mitra Energi Agung (MEA) to PT Niaga Gilang Persada, selling 3,060 shares worth IDR 15 billion, and MEA will no longer be a listed subsidiary. The divestment is part of IIR's business diversification strategy, focusing on more sustainable business, with the total value of 100% ownership over MEA reaching IDR 25 billion.
title: Indika Indonesia Resources divests 60% stake in Mitra Energri Agung to Niaga Gilang Persada for IDR 15 billion.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) has reached 75% of its target of 410 security issuances this year, with a diverse range of instruments including bonds, stocks, and asset-backed securities. IDX is committed to meeting its target and predicts it could even exceed 100% with remaining instruments in the pipeline, including structured warrants and bonds.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Reaches 75% of 410 Security Issuance Target This Year

body: PT Plaza Indonesia Realty Tbk will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 236.89 billion, or IDR 67 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on October 4, 2024. The company recorded a net profit of IDR 270.59 billion in the first half of 2024 and retained unrestricted earnings of IDR 9.71 trillion as of June 2024.
title: PT Plaza Indonesia Realty to Distribute IDR 236.89 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk has divested its steam power plant assets with a total capacity of 200 MW, generating proceeds that will be allocated towards sustainability sector investments, capital structure enhancement, and a share buyback plan to provide added value to shareholders. The divestment is part of TBS's commitment to reaching carbon neutrality by 2030, following a previous divestment in 2021 that yielded a profit of over USD 100 million.
title: TBS Energi Utama divests 200 MW steam power plant assets to support carbon neutrality goal.
